content:
Post 1

Flat index, wild internals. ASX 200 barely moved Thursday, closing at 8,840.70 — down just 0.40 points. But beneath the surface, AMP surged 9.83% while materials bled. The calm headline masked a full rotation. #ASX #ausbiz

Post 2

🟢 Financials +0.88%, Telcos +1.10%, Consumer Discretionary +1.09% led the charge. 🔴 Materials -1.58% and Energy -1.53% dragged. Classic risk rotation: defensives and yield plays in, commodities out. #ASX #ausbiz #banking #energy

Post 3

🔺 AMP surged 9.83% to AUD 1.90 — the session's standout mover and the clearest sign that financial stocks are attracting fresh conviction. REA Group added 6.61% to AUD 158.71, backing the consumer discretionary bid. #ASX #ausbiz #banking

Post 4

🪙 Gold at AUD 5,768.89/oz, silver AUD 81.81/oz. With materials off 1.58%, metals couldn't hold the sector up. AUD/USD sits at 0.6992 — a weaker Aussie supports gold in local terms but didn't stop the sector selloff. #gold #silver #ASX #metals

Post 5

ASX sits 3.94% off its 52-week high with materials under pressure. Watch gold at AUD 5,768/oz and whether Energy can recover from -1.53%. Bulls need commodities to stabilise. Do you see materials finding a floor this week? #ASX #ausbiz #gold #mining
